pr.title: mn: match mnitemsw, mnstagesw, and mnvibration functions
pr.author: itsgrimetime

summary: WIP closed/unmerged menu decomp PR that added matched or partially matched implementations and headers for item switch, stage switch, and vibration menu code. It introduced concrete functions in mnitemsw.c and mnstagesw.c, expanded mnvibration.c with several implemented functions plus placeholder markers, and added local/header structs for menu node traversal and asset/global declarations. Review focused on questionable type choices and code churn in mnvibration, especially wrapping HSD_GObj instead of using the real type and an apparent deletion/replacement of an existing function without explanation.
what_changed: Created mnitemsw implementations for mnItemSw_80233A98, mnItemSw_8023405C, and mnItemSw_802358C0, including language-sensitive item table lookup, linked-node traversal split at index 16, and menu transition setup for menu 0x10.
Added mnitemsw.h with ItemSwTable, public function prototypes, and externs for mnItemSw_803ED340 and mnItemSw_804D6BEC.

Created mnstagesw implementations for mnStageSw_8023593C, mnStageSw_802364A0, and mnStageSw_80237410, including stage unlock/apply logic through gm_801641CC, gm_80164430, gm_801641E4, linked-node traversal split at index 15, and menu transition setup for menu 0x11.
Added mnstagesw.h with public prototypes and externs for mnStageSw_803ED4C4 and mnStageSw_804D6BF4.

Expanded mnvibration.c by keeping MnVibrationAssets, adding mnVibration_802474C4 node traversal, fn_80248084 menu-guarded GObj unlink, fn_80248748 animation-end unlink logic, and a rewritten mnVibration_80249174 that loads vibration archive sections and creates a GObjProc.

Updated mnvibration.h from placeholder signatures to typed prototypes for mnVibration_802474C4, fn_80248084, fn_80248748, added VibNode/VibNodeContainer/VibData/VibJObjData structs, and declared mnVibration_803EECEC and mnVibration_804D6C28.
Replaced direct byte manipulation of a GObj flags byte in mnVibration_80249174 with use of the returned HSD_GObjProc and assignment to proc->flags_3 = HSD_GObj_804D783C.
PR was explicitly described by the author as WIP and was closed without merge.
smart_moves: The item and stage switch files reuse the same inferred linked-list node shape: padding to x8 next pointer and x10 child/head pointer, making the traversal pattern easy to recognize across adjacent menu systems.

The menu transition functions consistently update mn_804A04F0.prev_menu, cur_menu, and hovered_selection, then create a new GObjProc and assign proc->flags_3, which is cleaner than raw flag-byte patching.
mnItemSw_80233A98 preserves regional behavior by checking lbLang_IsSettingJP before returning alternate non-Japanese item IDs 0x27 and 0x28 for specific indices.
mnStageSw_8023593C uses the stage switch table mnStageSw_803ED4C4 and game-management functions rather than hardcoding stage state behavior beyond the observed loop bound.
mnvibration.h moved several placeholder declarations toward concrete types, improving cross-file usability where the evidence was available.
decomp_lessons: When an existing baselib type such as HSD_GObj is already known, prefer using it directly over inventing wrapper structs solely to reach offsets; reviewers questioned a wrapper type for GObj data access.
If a PR deletes or substantially rewrites an existing function while matching surrounding functions, call that out in the PR body or comments; unexplained removal triggered reviewer confusion.
For menu decomp, similar linked-node traversal code may recur across item switch, stage switch, and vibration screens, but each split index and container offset must be evidence-driven.
Use HSD_GObjProc return values and named fields such as flags_3 where available instead of raw pointer arithmetic against HSD_GObj internals.
Mark WIP decomp PRs clearly before review, especially when local structs, headers, or deleted functions are not yet cleaned up.
Header promotion is useful once function signatures, extern globals, and shared node/container structs are stable, but premature or overly local wrapper types can create review friction.
naming_conventions: Menu symbols use subsystem prefixes with address suffixes, e.g. mnItemSw_80233A98, mnStageSw_802364A0, mnVibration_802474C4.
Unidentified local callbacks remain fn_-address style, e.g. fn_80233E10, fn_80235F80, fn_80248084, fn_80248748.
Inferred structs in this slice use subsystem-specific names such as ItemSwNode, ItemSwData, StageSw_Node, StageSw_NodeData, VibNode, VibNodeContainer, VibData, and VibJObjData.
Global data and BSS-like symbols retain address suffixes in extern declarations, e.g. mnItemSw_803ED340, mnStageSw_803ED4C4, mnVibration_804D6C28.
Struct fields were mostly offset-style placeholders such as x8, x10, x2C, x34, x3C, and x50, reflecting unknown semantic names.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used explicit padding fields in inferred structs to place known pointers at exact offsets, such as ItemSwData.x34/x3C, StageSw_NodeData.x2C/x34, VibData.x2C, and VibNodeContainer.x50.
Matched list traversal by repeatedly null-checking the current node and advancing through x8, after first entering through a node's x10 child pointer.
Preserved loop variable widths as u8 in item/stage node traversal, likely to match generated code for index comparison and increment behavior.
Used a dummy local array in mnStageSw_8023593C with `(void) dummy;`, indicating a stack-shaping or matching artifact, though the exact reason is not explained in the PR context.
Used a function-pointer cast when calling mnVibration_80248ED4 from mnVibration_80249174 because the header still had an unresolved placeholder signature.
Converted direct GObj flag-byte manipulation to `proc->flags_3 = HSD_GObj_804D783C`, a cleaner match tactic when HSD_GObjProc layout is known.

review_feedback.author: PsiLupan
review_feedback.feedback: Questioned why the code made a wrapper type for a GObj instead of using HSD_GObj directly.
review_feedback.lesson: Do not introduce wrapper structs for known engine types without a strong matching or typing justification.

review_feedback.author: PsiLupan
review_feedback.feedback: Asked why an entire function was deleted.
review_feedback.lesson: Large deletions or function replacements need explicit explanation in WIP decomp PRs.

review_feedback.author: itsgrimetime
review_feedback.feedback: Author acknowledged the PR was still WIP and said they would clean the code up.
review_feedback.lesson: This slice should be treated as provisional; not all types or edits were review-ready.

follow_up_queries: Find the later PR or commit that replaced/finished PR #2077's WIP mnitemsw, mnstagesw, or mnvibration work.
Check whether VibNode, VibData, ItemSwNode, and StageSw_Node were later moved into shared mn/types.h or renamed with semantic field names.
Audit current mnvibration.c for whether HSD_GObj is used directly instead of a wrapper type as requested in review.
Compare later mnVibration_80249174 implementations to confirm the archive section load order and proc->flags_3 assignment.
Search for other menu switch functions that use the same linked-node traversal pattern with x8 next and x10 child pointers.
